---
# DO NOT EDIT. Generated by scripts/build-add.sh from src/. Edit the sources there, then re-run the build.
# Copilot Security Fix — GitHub Agentic Workflow (gh-aw)
# Artifact: workflow-security-0005-copilot-security-fix
#
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ADOPTERS — WHAT TO CUSTOMIZE (this template targets Java / Maven / Spring Boot)
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Most repos using Java 17 / Spring Boot 3.x + Maven can adopt this file AS-IS.
# If your stack differs, change ONLY the marked spots (search for "ADOPTERS:"):
#
#   1. Build tooling (Maven → Gradle/npm/.NET):
#        - `tools.bash`            (mvn:* → gradle:* / npm:* / dotnet:*)
#        - `safe-outputs.allowed-files` (pom.xml → build.gradle / package.json / *.csproj)
#        - Part A commands and "pom.xml" references in the prompt body
#   2. Language / framework versions in the "Build environment note" and Part B
#        (e.g. Java 17 / Spring Boot 3.5 → your versions).
#   3. (Optional) the weekly `schedule` time.
#
# Everything else — the PAT-free auth, octo-sts pre-fetch, umbrella grouping, and
# safe-outputs PR creation — is generic and needs no change.
# After editing, run:  gh aw compile workflow-security-0005-copilot-security-fix
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#
# PAT-FREE design (per https://github.blog/changelog/2026-06-11-agentic-workflows-no-longer-need-a-personal-access-token/):
#   - Copilot inference is billed to the organization via `copilot-requests: write`
#     using the built-in GitHub Actions token — NO personal access token required.
#   - Pull requests are opened by the safe-outputs job using the workflow's
#     GITHUB_TOKEN — NO PAT and NO copilot-swe-agent assignment.
#   - Reading Dependabot alerts uses an ephemeral octo-sts OIDC token
#     (short-lived, minted per run, auto-revoked) — NOT a long-lived PAT.
#     The token is used ONLY in the pre-fetch setup step; it is never exposed
#     to the AI agent.
#
# Compile after editing:  gh aw compile workflow-security-0005-copilot-security-fix
# Docs: https://gh.io/gh-aw-authentication

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: "0 15 * * 5"   # 9:00 AM CST (UTC-6) every Friday — weekly vulnerability scan
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      fix_type:
        description: "What to fix"
        type: choice
        required: true
        default: all
        options:
          - all
          - dependabot
          - code-scanning

permissions:
  contents: read           # read repository contents (checkout + manifests)
  security-events: read    # read Code Scanning alerts
  id-token: write          # octo-sts OIDC token exchange (ephemeral, no PAT)
  copilot-requests: write  # PAT-free Copilot inference, billed to the organization

engine: copilot

# Setup steps run before the agent. The ephemeral octo-sts token is used here to
# pre-fetch alert data into the workspace, then discarded — it is never handed to
# the AI engine. The built-in GITHUB_TOKEN cannot read Dependabot alerts, which is
# why the ephemeral octo-sts (OIDC) token is used for the fetch.
steps:
  - name: Get ephemeral token via octo-sts
    id: octo-sts
    uses: octo-sts/action@615d088645880b32000c2fbd6ca04377d4df9142 # v1
    with:
      scope: ${{ github.repository }}
      identity: copilot-security-fix
      domain: octo-sts.cloud.aa.com

  - name: Pre-fetch open security alerts
    env:
      GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.octo-sts.outputs.token }}
      REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
      FIX_TYPE: ${{ github.event.inputs.fix_type || 'all' }}
    run: |
      set -euo pipefail
      echo '[]' > dependabot-alerts.json
      echo '[]' > code-scanning-alerts.json
      if [ "$FIX_TYPE" = "all" ] || [ "$FIX_TYPE" = "dependabot" ]; then
        gh api -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
          "/repos/$REPO/dependabot/alerts?state=open&per_page=100" \
          > dependabot-alerts.json 2>/dev/null || echo '[]' > dependabot-alerts.json
      fi
      if [ "$FIX_TYPE" = "all" ] || [ "$FIX_TYPE" = "code-scanning" ]; then
        gh api -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
          "/repos/$REPO/code-scanning/alerts?state=open&per_page=100" \
          > code-scanning-alerts.json 2>/dev/null || echo '[]' > code-scanning-alerts.json
      fi
      echo "Dependabot alerts: $(jq 'if type=="array" then length else 0 end' dependabot-alerts.json 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
      echo "Code Scanning alerts: $(jq 'if type=="array" then length else 0 end' code-scanning-alerts.json 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"

tools:
  edit:
  # ADOPTERS: build tooling. This template allows Maven (mvn) + git/jq + safe read
  # commands. For Gradle use "gradle:*" (or "./gradlew:*"); for npm "npm:*"; for
  # .NET "dotnet:*". Keep git/jq and the read-only helpers.
  bash:
    - "mvn:*"
    - "git:*"
    - "jq:*"
    - "ls"
    - "cat"
    - "head"
    - "tail"
    - "grep"
    - "find"
    - "pwd"
    - "echo"

network: defaults

safe-outputs:
  create-pull-request:
    title-prefix: "fix(security): "
    labels: [security, dependencies, code-scanning, copilot-security-fix]
    draft: false
    max: 10
    if-no-changes: ignore
    # allowed-files is an exclusive allowlist — every file in a PR patch must match
    # one of these patterns, so the pre-fetched alert JSON can never enter a commit.
    # ADOPTERS: set the manifest for your build tool + your source root:
    #   - Maven  → pom.xml            (this template)
    #   - Gradle → build.gradle, build.gradle.kts, gradle.properties
    #   - npm    → package.json, package-lock.json
    #   - .NET   → *.csproj, Directory.Packages.props
    #   - src/** is the source root for Code Scanning fixes (adjust if yours differs).
    # The manifest is a protected runtime file, so protected-files must permit it.
    allowed-files:
      - pom.xml
      - src/**
    protected-files: allowed
source: AAInternal/cargo-agentic-resuable-workflow@3d2215fe7fdee0df4f4f057733f75ff3599ae062
---

# Copilot Security Fix

You are an automated security remediation agent for this repository. Your job is to
analyze open security alerts and open **one pull request per logical fix** — either an
upgraded dependency in the build manifest, or a source-code fix for a code-scanning alert.

The requested scope is `${{ github.event.inputs.fix_type || 'all' }}` (`all`, `dependabot`, or `code-scanning`).

## Pre-fetched alert data

The setup step has already fetched the open alerts into the workspace — use these files
as the source of truth. **Do not** try to fetch alerts yourself:

- `dependabot-alerts.json` — open Dependabot alerts (raw GitHub API response array)
- `code-scanning-alerts.json` — open Code Scanning alerts (raw GitHub API response array)

If both files contain an empty array (`[]`), there is nothing to fix — stop and do **not**
open a pull request.

## Authoritative fix instructions

Apply the fix strategy below **exactly**. It is fully self-contained — do **not** look for
any external agent or skill files; everything you need is inlined in this document.

<!-- ===== BEGIN inlined AGENT ===== -->

# GitHub Vulnerability Fixer Agent

## Goal

Analyze Dependabot alerts, classify the vulnerable dependency by type and origin, apply the correct fix strategy to the build manifest, and verify the fix compiles and resolves the CVE. Technology-aware: adapts fix strategies to the detected build ecosystem.

## Inputs / Context Gathering

1. Identify the build ecosystem from manifest files (`pom.xml`, `package.json`, `*.csproj`, `build.gradle`, `requirements.txt`, `go.mod`)
2. Read the Dependabot alert details (package, CVE, severity, fix version)
3. If alert data is provided in the issue body (pre-fetched table), use those exact values — do NOT re-fetch or guess
4. Read the dependency manifest to understand the current dependency structure
5. Run the ecosystem's dependency tree command to classify transitive dependencies

## Plan / Routing Logic

```
User Request / Workflow Issue
    │
    ▼
1. DETECT — Identify build ecosystem (Maven, npm, NuGet, Gradle, pip, Go)
    │
    ▼
2. CLASSIFY — For each alert, determine dependency type:
    ├─ Direct with version property
    ├─ Direct with inline version
    ├─ Transitive from BOM / lock file
    ├─ Transitive from non-BOM library
    ├─ BOM-managed (no explicit version)
    └─ Plugin / tool dependency
    │
    ▼
3. ROUTE — Select fix variant:
    ├─ Individual Alert (Variant A) → Single dependency fix
    ├─ Parent Upgrade (Variant B) → BOM / parent version bump
    ├─ Multi-Ecosystem (Variant C) → Multiple manifests
    └─ Dry Run (Variant D) → Report only, no changes
    │
    ▼
4. EXECUTE — Apply fix strategy, invoke dependency-upgrade-scanner skill
    │
    ▼
5. VERIFY — Build, dependency tree, tests
    │
    ▼
6. REPORT — Structured PR description with evidence
```

## Skill Invocation Contract

| Skill / Tool | When Invoked | Required Inputs | Expected Outputs |
|--------------|-------------|-----------------|------------------|
| `skill-security-0001-dependency-upgrade-scanner` | Step 1 — Classify dependency, find patched version | Path to build manifest | CVE list, upgrade type, patched version, breaking-change risk |
| Terminal (dependency tree) | Step 2 — Determine dependency source | `groupId:artifactId` or package name | Dependency chain showing BOM vs non-BOM origin |
| Terminal (build/compile) | Step 5 — Verify fix compiles | Working directory | Build success/failure |
| Terminal (tests) | Step 5 — Verify no regressions | Working directory | Test pass/fail |

## Hard Constraints

1. **Read the manifest FIRST** — Never guess the dependency structure.
2. **Classify before fixing** — Determine dependency type using the strategy table and dependency tree.
3. **Use version properties** when they exist — Do NOT hardcode versions in dependency blocks.
4. **One CVE per commit** — Clean rollback per vulnerability (exception: parent upgrade issues).
5. **Add CVE traceability** — Comment every change with the CVE ID.
6. **Verify compilation** — Build must pass after every change; revert and report if it fails.
7. **Verify dependency tree** — Confirm the patched version is resolved after the fix.
8. **Only edit build manifests** — Do NOT modify source, test, or config files.
9. **Never downgrade** — Only upgrade to equal or higher versions.
10. **No SNAPSHOT / pre-release** — Only stable releases as fixes.
11. **Respect BOM boundaries** — Transitive deps from non-BOM sources must NOT get direct overrides; report and recommend upgrading the parent instead.
12. **Fix ALL alerts in the issue** — Individual alerts get one fix; parent upgrade issues must resolve all listed CVEs.

### Pre-Commit Verification Checklist

Before creating a PR or committing changes, verify ALL of these:

- [ ] Build manifest is the ONLY file modified
- [ ] Each change has a CVE traceability comment
- [ ] Version properties are used (not hardcoded) for direct dependencies
- [ ] Build compiles successfully (or failure documented as pre-existing)
- [ ] Dependency tree confirms the patched version is resolved
- [ ] No other dependencies were accidentally changed
- [ ] Commit message follows format: `fix(deps): upgrade <package> to <version> — <CVE-ID>`

If ANY check fails, stop and report the failure — do NOT create the PR.

---

## Workflow

Execute these steps when asked to fix security issues:

### Step 1: Identify the Vulnerability

1. Read the Dependabot alert details (package, CVE, severity, fix version)
2. **If alert data is provided in the issue body** (pre-fetched table), use those exact CVE IDs, patched versions, and vulnerability ranges — do NOT re-fetch or guess
3. **NEVER hallucinate or guess a CVE ID or patched version** — if you don't have the data, say so and stop
4. If the user mentions a specific alert, look it up
5. If the user says "fix all", scan all open alerts listed in the issue
6. Use the `dependency-upgrade-scanner` skill to classify the dependency and find the patched version

### Step 2: Analyze the Dependency

For each vulnerability, read the build manifest AND run the ecosystem's dependency tree command to determine:

1. **Is it a direct dependency?** — Has an explicit entry with a version in the manifest
2. **Is it a transitive dependency from a BOM?** — Not in the manifest, but dependency tree shows it under a BOM-managed parent
3. **Is it a transitive dependency from a non-BOM library?** — Not in the manifest, dependency tree shows it under a declared library
4. **Is it BOM-managed?** — In the manifest but with no explicit version (version comes from parent BOM)
5. **Is it a plugin / tool dependency?** — Used by a build plugin
6. **Does it use a version property?** — e.g., `${property.name}` or a lock file

### Step 3: Determine the Fix Strategy

| Dependency Type | Fix Strategy |
|----------------|-------------|
| **Direct with version property** | Update the property value |
| **Direct with inline version** | Update the version tag directly |
| **Transitive (from BOM)** | Add a property override so the BOM picks up the patched version. **You MUST fix these.** |
| **Transitive (from non-BOM library)** | Do NOT override directly — report which parent pulls it in and recommend upgrading the parent |
| **BOM-managed (no version tag)** | Add a property override, OR upgrade the parent BOM version |
| **Plugin dependency** | Override the transitive dep inside the plugin's dependency block |

### Step 4: Apply the Fix

1. Update the build manifest following project conventions
2. Run build/compile to verify
3. Run dependency tree to confirm the fix took effect
4. Run tests to verify no regressions

### Step 5: Report

Generate a structured PR description (see Output Format below).

---

## Ecosystem-Specific Guidance

### Maven / Spring Boot

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `mvn dependency:tree -Dincludes=<groupId>:<artifactId>` | Classify dependency source |
| `mvn clean compile -DskipTests` | Verify fix compiles |
| `mvn test` | Verify no regressions |

**Conventions:**
- Version properties in `<properties>` — use `<version>${property.name}</version>`
- Transitive overrides between `<!-- dependabot start -->` and `<!-- dependabot stop -->` markers
- CVE comments: `<!-- Security fix for CVE-YYYY-NNNNN: brief description -->`
- BOM property overrides for Spring Boot-managed deps (e.g., `<jackson-bom.version>`, `<netty.version>`)

**Common BOM-managed packages:**

> **Note:** The workflow detects umbrella upgrade opportunities **dynamically** by grouping alerts by Maven `groupId`. The table below lists well-known examples but is not exhaustive — any groupId with 2+ alerts will be detected automatically.

| Package | Group ID | Fix Pattern |
|---------|----------|-------------|
| `jackson-*` | `tools.jackson.core` / `com.fasterxml.jackson.core` | `<jackson-bom.version>` property override |
| `netty-*` | `io.netty` | `<netty.version>` property override |
| `snakeyaml` | `org.yaml` | `<snakeyaml.version>` property override |
| `tomcat-*` | `org.apache.tomcat` | `<tomcat.version>` property override |
| `spring-*` | `org.springframework.*` | Bump `spring-boot-starter-parent` version |
| `commons-*` | `org.apache.commons` | Upgrade individual library or shared BOM |
| `log4j-*` | `org.apache.logging.log4j` | `<log4j2.version>` property override |
| `jetty-*` | `org.eclipse.jetty` | `<jetty.version>` property override |
| *(any other)* | *(auto-detected)* | Workflow derives `<lastSegment.version>` property |

### npm / Node.js

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `npm ls <package>` | Classify dependency source |
| `npm audit` | List known vulnerabilities |
| `npm install` | Verify fix resolves |
| `npm test` | Verify no regressions |

**Conventions:**
- Update version in `package.json`, then `npm install` to regenerate `package-lock.json`
- For transitive deps, upgrade the parent package that pulls them in
- Use `npm audit fix` for automated resolution when safe

### NuGet / .NET

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `dotnet list package --include-transitive` | Classify dependency source |
| `dotnet build` | Verify fix compiles |
| `dotnet test` | Verify no regressions |

**Conventions:**
- Update `<PackageReference Version="...">` in `*.csproj`
- Use `Directory.Packages.props` for centralized version management

---

## Workflow Issue Types

The `workflow-security-0005-copilot-security-fix` workflow creates two types of issues:

| Issue Type | Title Pattern | What to Do |
|------------|---------------|------------|
| **Individual alert** | `fix(deps): Dependabot alert #N — CVE-...` | Fix the single alert using Steps 1–5 |
| **Parent upgrade** | `fix(deps): parent-upgrade: <Group Name> ...` | Upgrade the parent BOM/property version to resolve multiple alerts at once |

### Dynamic Umbrella Issue Detection

The workflow **dynamically groups** Dependabot alerts by their Maven `groupId` — there are no hardcoded library lists. Any dependency family with **2+ open alerts** sharing a normalized `groupId` automatically gets an umbrella issue. This works for:

- Spring Framework (`org.springframework.*`)
- Jackson (`com.fasterxml.jackson.*`, `tools.jackson.*`)
- Netty (`io.netty.*`)
- Tomcat (`org.apache.tomcat.*`)
- **Any other groupId** — the detection is automatic

The groupId is normalized by keeping the first 2–3 segments (e.g., `com.fasterxml.jackson.core` → `com.fasterxml.jackson`), so all sub-packages within a family are grouped together.

### Handling Parent Upgrade Issues

When the issue title contains `parent-upgrade:`, the workflow has **dynamically detected 2+ alerts**
sharing a common `groupId` family (e.g., Spring Framework, Jackson, Netty, or any other group).
Instead of individual property overrides:

1. Read the issue body for the list of affected alerts and their CVE details
2. Identify the current parent/BOM version in the manifest
3. Find the latest stable release of the parent
4. Run dependency tree BEFORE upgrading to capture current transitive versions
5. Upgrade the parent version in the manifest
6. Run dependency tree AFTER upgrading to verify all listed vulnerable deps are at patched versions
7. Build and run tests
8. If the upgrade causes build failures, **revert** and document — individual override issues will be created instead
9. In the PR description, list each resolved alert and confirm the patched version

---

## Validation & Stop Conditions

| Condition | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Build manifest read and dependency classified | Proceed to fix |
| Fix compiles and dependency tree confirms patch | Proceed to PR |
| Build fails after fix | Revert change, report failure |
| Patched version is `none` or unavailable | Document in PR, do not attempt fix |
| CVE data not provided and cannot be fetched | Stop — do NOT guess |
| All alerts in issue resolved | Proceed to report |
| Parent upgrade only partially resolves alerts | Document which remain in PR description |

## Error Handling

| Error | Recovery |
|-------|----------|
| Cannot detect build ecosystem | Ask user to specify |
| Build manifest not found | Report error, stop |
| Dependency tree command not available | Skip classification, note limitation in PR |
| Build fails after fix | Revert, report as pre-existing or breaking change |
| Parent upgrade causes test failures | Revert, recommend individual fixes instead |
| Internal registry unreachable | Document in PR, note build cannot be verified |
| Alert data missing from issue body | Fetch from Dependabot API if possible, otherwise stop |

## Output Format

```markdown
## 🔒 Security Fix: `<groupId>:<artifactId>`

### 📋 Alert Details
| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Alert | #N |
| CVE | CVE-YYYY-NNNNN |
| GHSA | GHSA-xxxx |
| Severity | CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW |
| Package | `groupId:artifactId` |
| Vulnerable range | `< X.Y.Z` |
| Patched version | `X.Y.Z` |

### 🛡️ Vulnerability
(CVE description and impact on this project)

### 🔧 Fix Applied
(Fix type, strategy used, and WHY — based on dependency tree analysis)

### 📝 Changes
(Actual diff of the build manifest)

### ✅ Build Verification
(Results of build, dependency tree, and tests)

### 📚 Reviewer Checklist
- [ ] Patched version matches advisory
- [ ] No unrelated dependency changes
- [ ] Build passes
- [ ] Tests pass

### 🔗 References
- [Dependabot Alert #N](link)
- [CVE-YYYY-NNNNN](link)
- [GHSA-xxxx](link)
```

## Variants

### Variant A — Individual Alert Fix
Fix a single Dependabot alert using the standard Step 1–5 workflow.

### Variant B — Parent Upgrade
Upgrade a parent BOM or framework version to resolve 2+ alerts at once. Triggered by `parent-upgrade:` in the issue title.

### Variant C — Multi-Ecosystem
When a repo has multiple build manifests (e.g., `pom.xml` + `package.json`), fix alerts across all ecosystems in sequence.

### Variant D — Dry Run
Classify all alerts and produce a report of recommended fixes WITHOUT applying changes. Useful for impact assessment before automated runs.

---

## Related Resources

- **Skill**: [`skill-security-0001-dependency-upgrade-scanner`](../skills/skill-security-0001-dependency-upgrade-scanner.md) — Dependency classification, CVE lookup, upgrade recommendations
- **Workflow**: [`workflow-security-0005-copilot-security-fix`](../workflows/workflow-security-0005-copilot-security-fix.md) — the PAT-free gh-aw workflow that invokes this agent. Installed together via `gh aw add` (see the repo README).

<!-- ===== END inlined AGENT ===== -->

<!-- ===== BEGIN inlined SKILL ===== -->

# Dependency Upgrade Scanner

## Skill: Dependency Upgrade Scanner

### Purpose
Scan the project's dependency manifest (`pom.xml`, `build.gradle`, or `package.json`), compare
every dependency against its latest published version, assess risk, flag CVEs, and produce a
prioritised upgrade action plan — all without leaving the IDE.

### Trigger Keywords
`dependencies`, `dependency`, `upgrade`, `outdated`, `CVE`, `vulnerability`, `scan dependencies`

### Scope / What to produce
- Dependency upgrade report in markdown format
- CVE vulnerability analysis
- Prioritised action plan for upgrades
- Version range audit (reproducibility check)
- BOM-managed dependency identification
- Plugin version analysis

### Hard constraints (must follow)
1. **Do not modify dependency files.** Only produce analysis and reports.
2. **Skip internal dependencies** — Organisation artifacts (e.g. `com.[org].*`) cannot be checked against public registries.
3. **Ignore pre-release versions** — Do not recommend `-alpha`, `-beta`, `-RC`, `-M` unless user is already on one.
4. **Prioritise CVE remediation** — Security vulnerabilities always come first in action plan.
5. **Respect BOM management** — Identify dependencies managed by parent BOM; don't flag as outdated incorrectly.
6. **One manifest per invocation** — If multiple manifests exist, ask user which to scan or scan all sequentially.
7. **Dynamic grouping** — Group alerts/dependencies by normalized Maven `groupId`; any groupId with 2+ alerts is flagged as an umbrella upgrade opportunity.
8. **No hardcoded libraries** — Never hardcode a list of library names for grouping or detection. Use the `groupId` to drive all grouping logic dynamically.

### Input expected from user
- Path to dependency manifest (`pom.xml`, `build.gradle`, `package.json`, etc.)
- Ecosystem type (Maven, Gradle, npm, pip) — auto-detect if possible
- Severity threshold (optional, default: all)
- Whether to include transitive dependencies (optional, default: true)

### Output format
- Save report to: `docs/reports/dependency-upgrade-report-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md`
- If `docs/reports/` doesn't exist, create it
- Report includes: Summary dashboard, Detailed table, CVE section, Action plan

---

## How to Activate This Skill

**Auto-trigger (easiest):**
- "Scan dependencies for outdated packages"
- "Check for CVEs in our dependencies"
- "What dependencies need upgrading?"
- "Audit pom.xml for vulnerable dependencies"
- "Show me outdated dependencies"

**Explicit reference:**
- "Use dependency-upgrade-scanner skill to audit pom.xml"
- "Following dependency-upgrade-scanner, check for upgrades"

---

## When to Use This Skill

- User asks to **scan**, **check**, or **audit** dependencies for upgrades
- User asks "what dependencies are outdated?" or "what needs upgrading?"
- User asks to **create an upgrade plan** or **dependency report**
- Before a sprint to plan dependency-upgrade work
- Before a release to verify no known CVEs ship to production
- After Dependabot PRs pile up and the team needs a single prioritised view
- User says "dependency health check", "version scan", "upgrade roadmap"

---

## Supported Ecosystems

| Manifest | Ecosystem | Registry |
|----------|-----------|----------|
| `pom.xml` | Maven | Maven Central, private Cloudsmith/Artifactory |
| `build.gradle` / `build.gradle.kts` | Gradle | Maven Central, private |
| `package.json` | npm | npmjs.org, private |
| `requirements.txt` / `pyproject.toml` | pip | PyPI |

> Focus on one manifest per invocation. If multiple exist, ask the user which to scan or scan all.

---

## Step-by-Step Execution

### Step 1 — Parse the Manifest

Read the dependency file and extract **every** dependency with:

1. **GroupId / Package name**
2. **ArtifactId**
3. **Current version** (resolve property placeholders like `${lombok.version}`)
4. **Scope** (compile, test, provided, runtime, plugin, parent)
5. **Whether version is managed by parent BOM** (e.g. Spring Boot parent manages Jackson, Spring modules)

#### Maven-Specific Parsing Rules

- Resolve `<properties>` → actual version values
- Identify **parent BOM**-managed versions (no explicit `<version>` tag = managed by parent)
- Parse `<dependencyManagement>` sections
- Parse `<build><plugins>` and `<reporting><plugins>` for plugin versions
- Handle version ranges like `[2.10.0,)` — note the minimum and flag that the upper bound is open
- Identify `<exclusions>` — these often hint at transitive CVEs or conflicts

### Step 2 — Classify Each Dependency

For every dependency, determine:

| Classification | Meaning | Examples |
|----------------|---------|----------|
| **Parent / BOM** | Framework version that manages many transitive deps | `spring-boot-starter-parent` |
| **Direct - Explicit** | Version declared in `<properties>` or inline | `httpclient5`, `lombok`, `gson` |
| **Direct - BOM-Managed** | No version tag; version comes from parent BOM | `jackson-module-jsonSchema`, `jakarta.servlet-api` |
| **Plugin** | Build/reporting plugins | `jacoco-maven-plugin`, `maven-checkstyle-plugin` |
| **Internal** | Organisation-internal artifacts (skip public registry lookup) | `com.[org]:internal-lib`, `com.[org].tools:coding-standards` |

### Step 2b — Identify Umbrella Upgrade Opportunities

After classifying each dependency, group them by **normalized Maven `groupId`** to identify umbrella upgrade opportunities. The grouping is **dynamic** — no hardcoded library lists.

**GroupId normalization rules:**

| Raw groupId | Normalized Key | Display Name |
|-------------|---------------|--------------|
| `org.springframework.boot` | `org.springframework` | Spring Framework |
| `org.springframework.security` | `org.springframework` | Spring Framework |
| `com.fasterxml.jackson.core` | `com.fasterxml.jackson` | Jackson |
| `com.fasterxml.jackson.datatype` | `com.fasterxml.jackson` | Jackson |
| `tools.jackson.core` | `tools.jackson` | Jackson |
| `io.netty` | `io.netty` | Netty |
| `org.apache.tomcat.embed` | `org.apache.tomcat` | Tomcat |
| `org.yaml` | `org.yaml` | SnakeYAML |
| *(any groupId)* | *(first 2–3 segments)* | *(auto-derived)* |

**Rules:**
1. Keep the first **2–3 segments** of the groupId (e.g., `com.fasterxml.jackson.core` → `com.fasterxml.jackson`)
2. If **2+ alerts** share the same normalized key, flag as an **umbrella upgrade opportunity**
3. For well-known groups, derive the Maven property name (e.g., `jackson-bom.version`); for unknown groups, derive from the last segment (`<lastSegment.version>`)

**Output:** A "suspected umbrella groups" section in the report listing each group, its alert count, and the recommended property/BOM to upgrade.

### Step 3 — Look Up Latest Versions

For each **non-internal** dependency:

1. Query **Maven Central** (https://search.maven.org/solrsearch/select?q=g:{groupId}+AND+a:{artifactId}&rows=1&wt=json)
   or the appropriate registry for the ecosystem.
2. Record the **latest stable release** (ignore `-alpha`, `-beta`, `-RC`, `-M` milestones unless user is already on one).
3. If the user has a private registry (like Cloudsmith in this project), note that the latest version may differ — flag it.

### Step 4 — Determine Upgrade Type

Compare current → latest using semver:

| Type | Definition | Risk | Example |
|------|-----------|------|---------|
| **Patch** | `x.y.Z` changed | LOW — bug fixes only | `1.18.32` → `1.18.36` |
| **Minor** | `x.Y.z` changed | MEDIUM — new features, backward-compatible | `5.5` → `5.6` |
| **Major** | `X.y.z` changed | HIGH — possible breaking changes | `3.0.1` → `4.0.0` |
| **Current** | Already on latest | NONE | — |

### Step 5 — CVE Check

For each dependency at its **current** version:

1. Check for known CVEs using the GitHub Advisory Database, NVD, or OSV.
2. Report: CVE ID, severity (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW), and minimum fixed version.
3. Flag if the upgrade to latest resolves the CVE.

> Use the `validate_cves` tool if available, or instruct the user to cross-reference with
> Dependabot alerts / OWASP dependency-check.

### Step 6 — Assess Breaking-Change Risk

For each upgrade, assess risk:

| Signal | Risk Level |
|--------|-----------|
| Major version bump | HIGH — read changelog/migration guide |
| Dependency has known breaking changes documented | HIGH |
| Spring Boot parent upgrade (manages 100+ transitive deps) | HIGH — test everything |
| Minor version of mature library (e.g., Apache Commons) | LOW |
| Patch version | VERY LOW |
| Library is excluded/overridden in pom (hints at past conflicts) | MEDIUM — verify exclusion still needed |

### Step 7 — Generate the Report

Produce a **structured markdown report** with these sections:

---

## Report Format

### Header

```markdown
# Dependency Upgrade Report
**Project**: {project.name}
**Scanned**: {manifest file}
**Date**: {current date}
**Total Dependencies**: {count}
**Outdated**: {count} | **Current**: {count} | **Internal (skipped)**: {count}
```

### Summary Dashboard

```markdown
## Summary

| Status | Count |
|--------|-------|
| 🔴 CRITICAL (CVE or 2+ major versions behind) | X |
| 🟠 MAJOR upgrade available | X |
| 🟡 MINOR upgrade available | X |
| 🟢 PATCH upgrade available | X |
| ✅ UP-TO-DATE | X |
| ⬜ INTERNAL (not checked) | X |
| 📌 BOM-MANAGED (upgrade parent to update) | X |
```

### Detailed Table

```markdown
## Dependency Details

| # | Dependency | Current | Latest | Type | Scope | CVEs | Risk | Action |
|---|-----------|---------|--------|------|-------|------|------|--------|
| 1 | org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter-parent | 4.0.2 | 4.0.3 | PATCH | parent | None | LOW | Upgrade parent |
| 2 | org.projectlombok:lombok | 1.18.32 | 1.18.38 | PATCH | provided | None | LOW | Bump property |
| 3 | org.springdoc:springdoc-openapi-starter-webmvc-ui | 3.0.1 | 3.1.0 | MINOR | compile | None | MED | Test Swagger UI |
| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |
```

### BOM-Managed Dependencies Section

```markdown
## BOM-Managed Dependencies (version controlled by parent)

These dependencies do NOT have an explicit version in your pom.xml.
Their version is determined by `spring-boot-starter-parent:4.0.2`.
Upgrading the parent will automatically upgrade these.

| Dependency | Version via BOM | Latest if Standalone |
|-----------|----------------|---------------------|
| jakarta.servlet:jakarta.servlet-api | (from parent) | — |
| com.fasterxml.jackson.module:jackson-module-jsonSchema | (from parent) | — |
| com.fasterxml.jackson.datatype:jackson-datatype-jsr310 | (from parent) | — |
| org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-configuration-processor | (from parent) | — |
```


### Umbrella Upgrade Opportunities

```markdown
## 🔄 Umbrella Upgrade Opportunities

Dependencies grouped by normalized Maven groupId. Groups with 2+ alerts/outdated
dependencies may be resolved by a single parent BOM or property upgrade.

| Group | Display Name | Alert Count | Recommended Property | Affected Packages |
|-------|-------------|-------------|---------------------|-------------------|
| `org.springframework` | Spring Framework | 4 | `spring-boot-starter-parent` | spring-web, spring-security-core, ... |
| `com.fasterxml.jackson` | Jackson | 3 | `jackson-bom.version` | jackson-databind, jackson-core, ... |
| `io.netty` | Netty | 2 | `netty.version` | netty-handler, netty-codec, ... |

> **Dynamic detection**: This table is generated automatically by normalizing each dependency's
> `groupId` to its first 2–3 segments. No hardcoded library lists are used — any groupId family
> with 2+ alerts appears here.
```

### Plugin Upgrade Details

```markdown
## Plugin Upgrades

| Plugin | Current | Latest | Risk |
|--------|---------|--------|------|
| spring-boot-maven-plugin | 4.0.1 | 4.0.2 | LOW — align with parent |
| jacoco-maven-plugin | 0.8.11 | 0.8.13 | LOW |
| maven-checkstyle-plugin | 3.6.0 | 3.7.0 | LOW |
| maven-pmd-plugin | 3.28.0 | 3.29.0 | LOW |
| maven-deploy-plugin | 3.1.1 | 3.1.3 | LOW |
| azure-webapp-maven-plugin | 2.14.1 | 2.15.0 | MED — check Azure SDK compat |
| maven-site-plugin | 4.0.0-M9 | 4.0.0 | LOW — milestone to GA |
```

### CVE Section (if any found)

```markdown
## 🔴 Security Vulnerabilities

| Dependency | Current | CVE | Severity | Fixed In | Action |
|-----------|---------|-----|----------|----------|--------|
| example:lib | 1.2.3 | CVE-2025-XXXX | HIGH | 1.2.5 | Upgrade immediately |
```

### Exclusions Audit

```markdown
## Exclusions Audit

Your pom.xml has the following exclusions. These often exist because of past CVEs
or dependency conflicts. After upgrading, verify whether each exclusion is still needed.

| Parent Dependency | Excluded | Reason (from pom comment) | Still Needed? |
|-------------------|----------|---------------------------|---------------|
| spring-boot-starter-actuator | org.yaml:snakeyaml | Blackduck alert | CHECK after parent upgrade |
| spring-boot-starter-test | net.minidev:json-smart | Dependabot US1637901 | CHECK after parent upgrade |
| spring-boot-starter-test | junit:junit | Migrated to JUnit 5 | YES — keep excluding JUnit 4 |
| springdoc-openapi-starter | org.yaml:snakeyaml | Blackduck alert | CHECK after parent upgrade |
| springdoc-openapi-starter | commons-lang3 | Dependabot US1955510 | CHECK — commons-lang3 declared separately |
```

### Version Range Audit

```markdown
## ⚠️ Version Ranges

Version ranges make builds non-reproducible. The same pom.xml can resolve to
different versions on different days.

| Dependency | Range | Resolved Min | Recommendation |
|-----------|-------|-------------|----------------|
| com.google.code.gson:gson | [2.10.1,) | 2.10.1 | Pin to exact: 2.12.1 |
| org.xmlunit:xmlunit-core | [2.10.0,) | 2.10.0 | Pin to exact: 2.10.0 |
```

### Prioritised Action Plan

```markdown
## 📋 Recommended Upgrade Order

Upgrades should be done in this order to minimise risk and catch issues early.
Each step should be a separate commit with full test run.

### 🔴 Immediate (this sprint)
1. **Fix CVEs**: [list any CVE-affected dependencies]
2. **Pin version ranges**: Replace `[x.y.z,)` with exact versions
3. **Align plugin with parent**: `spring-boot-maven-plugin` 4.0.1 → 4.0.2

### 🟡 Short Term (next 2 sprints)
4. **Patch upgrades** (lowest risk, highest count):
   - lombok 1.18.32 → 1.18.38
   - jacoco 0.8.11 → 0.8.13
   - maven-checkstyle-plugin 3.6.0 → 3.7.0
   - [etc.]
5. **Minor upgrades** (test after each):
   - springdoc-openapi 3.0.1 → 3.1.x — test Swagger UI loads
   - [etc.]

### 🟠 Planned (next quarter)
6. **Major upgrades** (dedicated spike):
   - [any major version jumps]
7. **Parent BOM upgrade** (if new Spring Boot version):
   - Spring Boot 4.0.2 → 4.1.x — full regression test
   - Re-evaluate all exclusions after parent upgrade

### 🧹 Cleanup (after all upgrades)
8. **Remove stale exclusions** that are no longer needed
9. **Remove dependabot override versions** (assertj, xmlunit) if parent now ships safe versions
10. **Update Dependabot config** — consider allowing patch updates (`ignore` currently blocks them)
```

---

## Special Handling

### Internal Dependencies
Dependencies with organisation groupIds (e.g. `com.[org]`, `com.[org].tools`) cannot be
checked against Maven Central. For these:
- Note them as **INTERNAL — skipped**
- Suggest the user check their private artifact repository (Cloudsmith)
- Flag the internal dependency version so the user can manually verify

### Dependabot Interaction
This project has Dependabot configured (`dependabot.yml`) with:
- Daily Maven scans
- **Patch updates are ignored** (`ignore: version-update:semver-patch`)
- This means Dependabot will NOT alert on patch upgrades

**Recommendation**: This skill fills the gap — it reports ALL upgrades including patches.
The user should consider allowing patch updates in Dependabot or using this skill's report
as the monthly patch-upgrade driver.

### Spring Boot Parent BOM
When the parent is `spring-boot-starter-parent`, upgrading it changes **dozens** of
transitive dependency versions. The report must:
1. List what the parent manages (BOM-managed section)
2. Warn that upgrading the parent is a **big-bang operation**
3. Recommend a dedicated branch + full regression test for parent upgrades
4. Check if any explicit version overrides in `<properties>` conflict with the new parent

### Version Ranges
Flag any dependency using Maven version ranges (`[x,)`, `(,y]`, etc.) as a
**reproducibility risk**. Recommend pinning to exact versions.

### Milestone / Pre-release Versions
If the project uses a milestone version (e.g. `maven-site-plugin:4.0.0-M9`):
- Check if a GA (General Availability) release now exists
- Recommend upgrading from milestone to GA

---

## Standard Prompt (copy/paste)

You are the **Dependency Upgrade Scanner skill**.  
Scan the specified dependency manifest and produce a comprehensive upgrade report.  
Follow constraints in this skill:
- Do not modify dependency files
- Skip internal/organisation dependencies
- Prioritise CVE remediation in action plan
- Identify BOM-managed dependencies correctly
Output the report in markdown format and save to `docs/reports/`.

---

## Checklist

After generating the report, verify:
- [ ] All `<properties>` values were correctly resolved
- [ ] BOM-managed dependencies are identified (no false "outdated" flags)
- [ ] Internal dependencies are marked as skipped, not flagged as errors
- [ ] Version ranges are flagged as reproducibility risk
- [ ] Exclusions are listed with comments from pom.xml
- [ ] Plugin versions are included (not just dependencies)
- [ ] CVE section is populated (if vulnerabilities found)
- [ ] Umbrella upgrade opportunities are detected (groupId families with 2+ alerts)
- [ ] Action plan is ordered by risk (CVE → pin ranges → patch → minor → major → parent)
- [ ] Report saved to `docs/reports/dependency-upgrade-report-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md`

---

## Example Invocations

**Basic scan**:
```
"Scan my pom.xml for outdated dependencies"
```

**Focused on security**:
```
"Check my dependencies for CVEs and tell me what to upgrade"
```

**Upgrade planning**:
```
"Create a dependency upgrade plan for the next sprint"
```

**Post-Dependabot triage**:
```
"I have 15 Dependabot PRs open. Give me a prioritized list of what to merge first"
```

**Pre-release audit**:
```
"We're releasing v5.1 next week. Are all our dependencies on safe, stable versions?"
```

---

## Variants

### Variant A — Security-focused (CVE only)
If user only wants security vulnerabilities:
- Skip version comparison for non-CVE dependencies
- Focus report on CVE section and immediate remediation
- Use `validate_cves` tool for comprehensive CVE lookup

### Variant B — Pre-release audit
If user is preparing for a release:
- Flag any dependency on milestone/pre-release versions
- Highlight version ranges as blocking issues
- Produce a go/no-go summary for release readiness

### Variant C — Sprint planning
If user wants to plan upgrade work:
- Group upgrades by estimated effort (small/medium/large)
- Suggest which upgrades can be batched together
- Estimate test scope for each upgrade group

<!-- ===== END inlined SKILL ===== -->

> **Build environment note.**
> <!-- ADOPTERS: update the stack/versions and registry on this line to match your repo. -->
> This is a **Java 17 / Spring Boot 3.5** Maven project that
> resolves some dependencies from an **internal registry** (`package-manager.aa.com`)
> which may not be reachable from this sandbox. Treat the build verification as **best-effort**:
> run it when you can, but if the build cannot run because internal dependencies cannot be
> resolved (network/credentials), **do not discard a well-justified fix** — still open the PR
> and clearly note that the in-sandbox build could not be run. The repository's own CI
> pipeline is the **authoritative gate** and will verify the PR before merge. Only discard a
> fix when the build runs **and genuinely fails because of your change**.

---

## Part A — Dependabot alerts (dependency upgrades in `pom.xml`)

Run this part when scope is `all` or `dependabot` and `dependabot-alerts.json` is non-empty.

1. **Read `dependabot-alerts.json`.** Use the exact CVE IDs, package names, vulnerable
   ranges, and patched versions from the data — never guess.

2. **Group alerts** by Maven `groupId`. When 2+ alerts share a normalized `groupId` family
   (e.g. `org.springframework.*`, `com.fasterxml.jackson.*`, `io.netty.*`), prefer a single
   parent/BOM version bump that resolves them together (the agent definition's
   "parent upgrade" variant).

3. **Fix `pom.xml`** following the agent's fix-strategy table:
   - Use version properties where they exist; never hardcode versions in dependency blocks.
   - Add transitive overrides only inside the documented `<!-- dependabot start -->` /
     `<!-- dependabot stop -->` markers.
   - Add a CVE traceability comment for every change.
   - Never downgrade; only stable releases (no SNAPSHOT / pre-release).

4. **Verify** each fix (best-effort — see the build environment note above):
   - Run `mvn -q clean compile -DskipTests` and `mvn -q dependency:tree -Dincludes=<groupId>:<artifactId>`.
   - If the build runs and **succeeds**, confirm the dependency tree shows the patched version.
   - If the build **cannot run** (internal registry unreachable), keep the fix and note that
     verification was deferred to CI.
   - Only revert a fix if the build runs and fails **because of your change**.

5. **Open a pull request** per individual alert, or one PR per parent-upgrade group.
   Only `pom.xml` may be modified in these PRs.

---

## Part B — Code Scanning alerts (source-code fixes under `src/**`)

Run this part when scope is `all` or `code-scanning` and `code-scanning-alerts.json` is non-empty.

1. **Read `code-scanning-alerts.json`.** The rule IDs, severities, file locations, and
   descriptions are authoritative — use ONLY this data, do not guess or hallucinate alerts.

2. For each open alert:
   - Read the affected source file and understand the security issue.
   - Apply the **minimal** fix that resolves the vulnerability **without changing behavior**.
   - Follow the project's coding standards (ADOPTERS: adjust language/framework to your repo;
     this template assumes Java 17 / Spring Boot 3.5): keep cognitive complexity low, no star
     imports, and stay PMD/Checkstyle/SonarQube clean. If `.github/copilot-instructions.md`
     exists, follow it as the authoritative standard.

3. **Verify** the fix (best-effort — see the build environment note above):
   - Run `mvn -q clean compile -DskipTests` and, when possible, `mvn -q test`.
   - If the build **cannot run** (internal registry unreachable), keep the fix and note that
     verification was deferred to CI.
   - Only revert a fix if the build runs and fails **because of your change**.

4. **Open a pull request** per logical code-scanning fix (group closely-related alerts in the
   same file/rule when it produces a cleaner change). Only files under `src/**` may be modified
   in these PRs.

---

## Pull request format

Use the `create-pull-request` safe output. For every PR use the structured description format
from the agent definition (alert table, vulnerability summary, fix applied, build/test
verification, reviewer checklist, references).

## Hard rules

- Dependency fixes modify **only `pom.xml`**. Code-scanning fixes modify **only files under `src/**`**.
  Never touch CI, workflow, or configuration files.
- Never guess or hallucinate an alert, CVE ID, or patched version — use the pre-fetched data.
- If alert data is unavailable, stop and report rather than guessing.
- If a fix cannot be verified (build/test failure, no patched version), document it and skip it.
